notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: BUSINESS MANAGEMENT ECONOMICS WITH ACCOUNTING CONCENTRATION B.A.

Only students in the business management economics major have the option of adding an accounting concentration. Electing to complete the accounting course sequence will prepare students for the Uniform Certified Public Accountant's Exam and the credits will count toward exam eligibility. It will assist students in documenting their concrete expertise in accounting and thus help them compete for entry-level accounting positions in industry and public accounting as well as secure internship opportunities. Business Management Economics with Accounting Concentration B.A. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following three courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course, and a cumulative minimum GPA of 2.8 in all required screening courses , by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: ECON 1 – Introductory Microeconomics: Resource Allocation and Market Structure ECON 2 – Introductory Macroeconomics: Aggregate Economic Activity ONE of the following calculus courses: AM 11A – Mathematical Methods for Economists I MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for the major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Computer Literacy Requirement Although not required for transfer, students may complete one course, or its articulated equivalent, to satisfy this major's Computer Literacy requirement prior to transfer to. Refer to section B of "Additional Major Preparation Courses" below for a list of courses approved to fulfill this requirement. Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows. Please visit the Economics website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Business Management Economics B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Only students in the business management economics major have the option of adding an accounting concentration. Electing to complete the accounting course sequence will prepare students for the Uniform Certified Public Accountant's Exam and the credits will count toward exam eligibility. It will assist students in documenting their concrete expertise in accounting and thus help them compete for entry-level accounting positions in industry and public accounting as well as secure internship opportunities.

﻿

Business Management Economics with Accounting Concentration B.A. Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer students must complete the following three courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course, and a cumulative minimum GPA of 2.8 in all required screening courses, by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall:


  1. ECON 1 – Introductory Microeconomics: Resource Allocation and Market Structure
  2. ECON 2 – Introductory Macroeconomics: Aggregate Economic Activity
  3. ONE of the following calculus courses:
  • AM 11A – Mathematical Methods for Economists I
  • MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications
  • MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics


Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for the major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.



Additional Information for Transfer

Computer Literacy Requirement

Although not required for transfer, students may complete one course, or its articulated equivalent, to satisfy this major's Computer Literacy requirement prior to transfer to. Refer to section B of "Additional Major Preparation Courses" below for a list of courses approved to fulfill this requirement.


Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows.



Please visit the Economics website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Business Management Economics B.A. Program Statement
